paragraphs.summary: This supervisory statement is addressed to all UK insurance firms within the scope of Solvency II, and to the Society of Lloyd’s, that carry out life insurance business or have annuities stemming from

The Solvency II supervisory reporting Implementing Technical Standards expects firms to use a defined list of product codes where attributed by the supervisory authority in the instructions to the tem

sections.chapter_title: Codes for firms that carry out life insurance business or have annuities stemming from non-life insurance business

paragraphs.summary: This supervisory statement sets out how the Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) expects firms to submit information in annual reporting QRT S.14.01 using life product reporting codes. The [Appendix]

In these product codes the third digit of the code is used to distinguish varieties of the same product. This separates conventional and unitised with-profit, unit-linked and index-linked as well as r

Miscellaneous codes have only been assigned for protection business where there are minor products that do not fit into the named codes. A firm may use code [624](/guidance/supervisory-statements/ss36

sections.chapter_title: The PRA’s expectations relating to instructions for templates S.14.01

paragraphs.summary: To achieve consistency of reporting between firms the PRA sets out in this section how it expects firms to interpret the instructions to the template S.14.01.

Where firms show the number of contracts in C0040 and C0050 the PRA expects firms to count multiple policies issued as part of the same premium, identifiable increments and rider benefits as being a s
For products which are reported with a ‘collective’ classification in C0100 the number of contracts to be reported in C0040 and C0050 is the number of members of the scheme where this is known to the
Where a product has been written in both single life and joint life form the product classification in C0100 is ‘5 – other’.

Where at least one version of a product is available to new policyholders it should be shown in C0130 as ‘1’. C0130 is ‘2’ for run-off which includes products only available as new business due to opt

Where premiums cease for a regular premium product the contract C0140 should continue to be reported as ‘1 – Regular premiums’. Where a product allows a flexible combination of regular and single prem

The PRA expects that the first three characters of the homogeneous risk group in C0170 and C0230 will be one of the three digit codes in the [Appendix](/guidance/supervisory-statements/ss36-15---solve
Firms should use an approximation to apportion between product codes where parts of the technical provisions are calculated for a combination of products (eg with-profits guarantee costs) or if there
The PRA does not expect that the guidance in this SS on the reporting of homogeneous risk groups would impact the calculation of the Transitional Measures on Technical Provisions.
